generatedSentence: Elara, a retired journalist and former adjunct to the empire's apparatchik, watched her jawfish swim. She documented the societal fallout in her journals, noting the distinct layers of discontent. Her android majordomo brought synthetic sweets. Elara knew the economic system was poorly managed, threatening a lasting lockdown. She struggled to obviate the dread in her chamber. Anybody could feel it. Likewise, a peculiar vagabond in a neckerchief often lingered anywhere near her old bakehouse. Her late grandmother had warned against notoriety. One day, the city's gazette reported that the empire's sockeye resources were being injected into war machines. Elara felt a ruffle of concern; this could outstay the peace. A shadowy hitman was rumored to be targeting testers exposing truth. She recalled her engagement to a champion goalkeeper, his gallant principles like a westerns hero, feeling a pang of envy. The machines were filling maximum quotas, increment by increment. She heard whooping and waving protestors, but knew the empire would simply accredit their dissent as random noise, then unleash rockets. The conflict climaxed during a housewarming party, where a flamboyant flamingo dancer performed ballet onstage. Elara spotted the hitman, a tomcat with a twisted katana, planning to unhand the dancer. A bystander, an ent, looked on. Elara, a ladybug vibrissa twitching, knew she had to act. She lifted a heavy jawbone from a display, swung it clockwise, and with a painful whacks and fracks, busted the hitman's weapon. His surprise was complete. Crikey! His plan was crushed; her painting of a lioness on the wall seemed to roar acceptance. The hitman, defeated, was unhand and accepting his fate. Elara had obviated a painful disaster. The aftermath felt like an idyll, a return to fragrant cinnamon peace, despite the asbestos fallout. The wealth of everything, from the plunked nickels she freely gave to the once-unanswered questions about the empire’s geography, now felt clearer. Her thorough vetting of the empire’s twisted facticity had finally paid off. She thought of a turkey dinner, a warm encore, as she watched the last of the machines being unplugged. No more fidget or tilt; just calm.
panelSentences: Elara, a retired journalist and former adjunct to the empire's apparatchik, watched her jawfish swim. She documented the societal fallout in her journals, noting the distinct layers of discontent. Her android majordomo brought synthetic sweets. Elara knew the economic system was poorly managed, threatening a lasting lockdown. She struggled to obviate the dread in her chamber. Anybody could feel it. Likewise, a peculiar vagabond in a neckerchief often lingered anywhere near her old bakehouse. Her late grandmother had warned against notoriety.

One day, the city's gazette reported that the empire's sockeye resources were being injected into war machines. Elara felt a ruffle of concern; this could outstay the peace. A shadowy hitman was rumored to be targeting testers exposing truth. She recalled her engagement to a champion goalkeeper, his gallant principles like a westerns hero, feeling a pang of envy. The machines were filling maximum quotas, increment by increment. She heard whooping and waving protestors, but knew the empire would simply accredit their dissent as random noise, then unleash rockets.

The conflict climaxed during a housewarming party, where a flamboyant flamingo dancer performed ballet onstage. Elara spotted the hitman, a tomcat with a twisted katana, planning to unhand the dancer. A bystander, an ent, looked on. Elara, a ladybug vibrissa twitching, knew she had to act. She lifted a heavy jawbone from a display, swung it clockwise, and with a painful whacks and fracks, busted the hitman's weapon. His surprise was complete. Crikey! His plan was crushed; her painting of a lioness on the wall seemed to roar acceptance.

The hitman, defeated, was unhand and accepting his fate. Elara had obviated a painful disaster. The aftermath felt like an idyll, a return to fragrant cinnamon peace, despite the asbestos fallout. The wealth of everything, from the plunked nickels she freely gave to the once-unanswered questions about the empire’s geography, now felt clearer. Her thorough vetting of the empire’s twisted facticity had finally paid off. She thought of a turkey dinner, a warm encore, as she watched the last of the machines being unplugged. No more fidget or tilt; just calm.
rawPanelSentences: Elara, a retired journalist and former adjunct to the empire's apparatchik, watched her jawfish swim. She documented the societal fallout in her journals, noting the distinct layers of discontent. Her android majordomo brought synthetic sweets. Elara knew the economic system was poorly managed, threatening a lasting lockdown. She struggled to obviate the dread in her chamber. Anybody could feel it. Likewise, a peculiar vagabond in a neckerchief often lingered anywhere near her old bakehouse. Her late grandmother had warned against notoriety.

One day, the city's gazette reported that the empire's sockeye resources were being injected into war machines. Elara felt a ruffle of concern; this could outstay the peace. A shadowy hitman was rumored to be targeting testers exposing truth. She recalled her engagement to a champion goalkeeper, his gallant principles like a westerns hero, feeling a pang of envy. The machines were filling maximum quotas, increment by increment. She heard whooping and waving protestors, but knew the empire would simply accredit their dissent as random noise, then unleash rockets.

The conflict climaxed during a housewarming party, where a flamboyant flamingo dancer performed ballet onstage. Elara spotted the hitman, a tomcat with a twisted katana, planning to unhand the dancer. A bystander, an ent, looked on. Elara, a ladybug vibrissa twitching, knew she had to act. She lifted a heavy jawbone from a display, swung it clockwise, and with a painful whacks and fracks, busted the hitman's weapon. His surprise was complete. Crikey! His plan was crushed; her painting of a lioness on the wall seemed to roar acceptance.

The hitman, defeated, was unhand and accepting his fate. Elara had obviated a painful disaster. The aftermath felt like an idyll, a return to fragrant cinnamon peace, despite the asbestos fallout. The wealth of everything, from the plunked nickels she freely gave to the once-unanswered questions about the empire’s geography, now felt clearer. Her thorough vetting of the empire’s twisted facticity had finally paid off. She thought of a turkey dinner, a warm encore, as she watched the last of the machines being unplugged. No more fidget or tilt; just calm.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A determined elderly woman with a single, glowing cybernetic eye, wearing a high-collared, patched-up trench coat over a practical jumpsuit. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist Elara, observed from a low angle, watching a neon-lit tank of jawfish, her cybernetic eye reflecting the glow. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: An extreme wide shot of a futuristic cityscape, rockets launching in the background, a large digital gazette headline about 'sockeye' and 'machines' visible on a skyscraper screen. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A dynamic eye-level shot of Elara, mid-swing with a large jawbone, deflecting a stylized katana wielded by a shadowy hitman, a flamingo dancer frozen mid-ballet in the background. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: A close-up, high-angle shot of Elara, looking thoughtful and content, holding the jawbone, with a holographic 'all clear' symbol subtly shimmering nearby.

definitions.definition: Any of many marine fish, of the family Opistognathidae, that have large mouths.
The bone of the lower jaw; the mandible.
Any of the bones in the lower or upper jaw.
(singular or plural) A shaken musical instrument (an idiophone) made from the jawbone of an animal and shaken such that the teeth vibrate in their sockets to produce sound.
To talk persistently in an attempt to persuade somebody to cooperate.
A single thickness of some material covering a surface.
definitions.example: After the first coat of paint dried, he applied another layer.

definitions.definition: A (usually) horizontal deposit; a stratum.
definitions.example: I find seven-layer cake a bit too rich.

definitions.definition: One of the items in a hierarchy.
definitions.example: mired in layers of deceit

definitions.definition: (by analogy to a stack of transparencies) one in a stack of (initially transparent) drawing surfaces that comprise an image; used to keep elements of an image separate so that they can be modified independently from one another.
To cut or divide (something) into layers
To arrange (something) in layers.
definitions.example: Layer the ribbons on top of one another to make an attractive pattern.

definitions.definition: A person who lays things, such as tiles.
A mature female bird, insect, etc. that is able to lay eggs.
definitions.example: When dealing with an infestation of headlice, the first step is to eliminate the layers.

definitions.definition: A hen kept to lay eggs.
A shoot of a plant, laid underground for growth.
A member of the Soviet apparat; a Communist bureaucrat or agent.
A blindly loyal bureaucrat.
An appendage; something attached to something else in a subordinate capacity.
A person associated with another, usually in a subordinate position; a colleague.
An unmalted grain or grain product that supplements the main mash ingredient.
A quality or property of the body or mind, whether natural or acquired, such as colour in the body or judgement in the mind.
A key or scale closely related to another as principal; a relative or attendant key.
(grammar) A dispensable phrase in a clause or sentence that amplifies its meaning, such as "for a while" in "I typed for a while".
(X-bar theory) A constituent which is both the daughter and the sister of an X-bar.
Symploce.
One of a pair of morphisms which relate to each other through a pair of adjoint functors.
Connected in a subordinate function.
Added to a faculty or staff in a secondary position.
Any one out of an indefinite number of persons; anyone; any person.
definitions.example: Anybody will do.

definitions.definition: A person of some consideration or standing.
definitions.example: Everybody who wants to be anybody will come to Jake's party.

definitions.definition: To stop working on a permanent basis, usually because of old age or illness.
definitions.example: Having made a large fortune, he retired.

definitions.definition: (sometimes reflexive) To withdraw; to take away.
To cease use or production of something.
definitions.example: The steamship made thousands of trips over several decades before it was retired by the shipping company.

definitions.definition: To withdraw from circulation, or from the market; to take up and pay.
definitions.example: The central bank retired those notes five years ago.

definitions.definition: To cause to retire; specifically, to designate as no longer qualified for active service; to place on the retired list.
definitions.example: The board retired the old major.

definitions.definition: (of a batsman) To voluntarily stop batting before being dismissed so that the next batsman can bat.
definitions.example: Jones retired in favour of Smith.

definitions.definition: (of a fielder) To make a play which results in a runner or the batter being out, either by means of a put out, fly out or strikeout.
definitions.example: Jones retired Smith 6-3.

definitions.definition: To go back or return; to withdraw or retreat, especially from public view; to go into privacy.
definitions.example: I will retire to the study.

definitions.definition: To retreat from action or danger; to withdraw for safety or pleasure.
definitions.example: The regiment retired from the fray after the Major was killed.

definitions.definition: To recede; to fall or bend back.
definitions.example: Past the point, the shore retires into a sequence of coves.

definitions.definition: To go to bed.
definitions.example: I will retire for the night.

definitions.definition: To remove or cease to use.
definitions.example: When a hurricane becomes so deadly or destructive that future use would be insensitive, officials may retire the name of the hurricane.

definitions.definition: To fit (a vehicle) with new tires.
Secluded from society (of a lifestyle, activity etc.); private, quiet.
Of a place: far from civilisation, not able to be easily seen or accessed; secluded.
(of people) Having left employment, especially on reaching pensionable age.
definitions.example: The retired workers are a major expense due to their pensions.

definitions.definition: No longer in use or production.
definitions.example: Following Jackie Robinson's success, his uniform number, 42, became a retired number across all major league teams.

definitions.definition: Any gathered or curled strip of fabric added as trim or decoration.
definitions.example: She loved the dress with the lace ruffle at the hem.

definitions.definition: Disturbance; agitation; commotion.
definitions.example: to put the mind in a ruffle

definitions.definition: A low, vibrating beat of a drum, quieter than a roll; a ruff.
The connected series of large egg capsules, or oothecae, of several species of American marine gastropods of the genus Fulgur.
To make a ruffle in; to curl or flute, as an edge of fabric.
definitions.example: Ruffle the end of the cuff.

definitions.definition: To disturb; especially, to cause to flutter.

definitions.definition: To grow rough, boisterous, or turbulent.
To become disordered; to play loosely; to flutter.
To be rough; to jar; to be in contention; hence, to put on airs; to swagger.
To make into a ruff; to draw or contract into puckers, plaits, or folds; to wrinkle.

definitions.definition: Confectionery, candy.

definitions.example: On 26 April 1986 the reactor number four at the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant located in the Soviet Union near Pripyat in Ukraine exploded. Further explosions and the resulting fire sent a plume of highly radioactive fallout into the atmosphere and over an extensive geographical area.

definitions.definition: A negative side effect; an undesirable or unexpected consequence.

definitions.definition: A declined offer in a sales transaction when acceptance was presumed.

definitions.definition: A computer.

definitions.definition: A person or organisation that seemingly acts like a machine, being particularly efficient, single-minded, or unemotional.

definitions.definition: Especially, the group that controls a political or similar organization; a combination of persons acting together for a common purpose, with the agencies which they use.

To abandon, give up (someone or something).
To move from side to side; to sway.

Floating about without any certain direction; driven to and fro.
To open (something that was clenched).

definitions.definition: To relax, especially one's muscles.

definitions.definition: In or at any location or an unknown location.

definitions.definition: To (in the direction of) any location or an unknown location.
definitions.example: Anywhere you go in France, there will be bread and wine with dinner.

definitions.definition: Any location or an unknown location.
definitions.example: Anywhere is better than here!

definitions.definition: A brief extra performance, done after the main performance is complete.

A technical error of touching or surpassing the starting mark prior to the official start signal in the sporting events of long jump, discus, hammer throw, shot put, and similar. Originally the starting mark was a scratch on the ground but is now a board or precisely indicated mark.
The last riders to depart in a handicap race.
An aberration.
A horse withdrawn from a race prior to the start.

definitions.definition: Money.
A feed, usually a mixture of a few common grains, given to chickens.

A scratch wig.
A genre of Virgin Islander music, better known as fungi.

Speed, full speed; impetuosity, force.

An undefined, unknown or unimportant person; a person of no consequence.

(somewhat colloquial) Representative and undistinguished; typical and average; selected for no particular reason.

definitions.definition: (somewhat colloquial) Apropos of nothing; lacking context; unexpected; having apparent lack of plan, cause or reason.

definitions.definition: Connection or attachment.
